@DarkEndMoon1 @mrddmia @RonColeman I'm not saying that wouldn't be a good rule to have. I am saying that the claim that it's obvious that it's the case seems quite absurd without some citation to some authority holding so.

@DarkEndMoon1 @mrddmia @RonColeman It is not obvious to me that given that Congress has the power to create or destroy courts inferior to the Supreme Court, they nevertheless don't have the power to monitor with little or no restriction what goes on in those inferior courts.

@dogg_not @RAHarrisonPA @RobertFreundLaw @DrSarteschi Also, to address the libel issue more directly, we don't hold people liable for libel absent some evidence of wrongdoing. If, with no wrongdoing, I libel you and cause you severe damages, you still have no remedy at law in the United States.

Why do we do this? Because we don't
@mrddmia @RonColeman Do you have any citation to precedential holdings that make it obvious that they couldn't do so even if a statute says they can? It's certainly not obvious to me.
@reeeesistance These guys get a pass though. https://t.co/F5aWGtFWG7

@dogg_not @RAHarrisonPA @RobertFreundLaw @DrSarteschi And it's quite possible that speaking by embedding your views in an AI could become (some might argue, already is) one of the most effective ways to spread ideas and advocate for them. It is likely no less important as a form of speech as television and the internet.

probability.last_reasoning: Bear case increased from 10% to 14%: Hormuz closure confirmed, active military escalation with NATO fracture (Spain), compound stress upgrading to ELEVATED, extreme fear at 14, and XRP at $1.36 already below the bear case threshold. Mid case increased from 38% to 33% net shift: the Ripple Payments expansion and JoelKatz hints support institutional pipeline, but execution is delayed by macro chaos. Base case slightly increased to 42% as the most likely outcome in a prolonged geopolitical uncertainty environment. Bull case trimmed to 11% — harder to reach $20+ when the global system is under active stress even though stress paradoxically validates the settlement infrastructure thesis.

bear_case.score_reasoning: Score elevated from prior period. Three drivers: (1) active geopolitical crisis creating real economic disruption, not theoretical risk; (2) XRP price at $1.36 — already below many base case entry assumptions — in extreme fear environment; (3) institutional adoption metrics (ODL, permissioned DEX, ETF AUM) still lacking hard data while competing infrastructure (Visa/Stripe stablecoin cards, DTCC digital assets, Ondo tokenized securities) is shipping product.

competing_infrastructure.name: SWIFT GPI
competing_infrastructure.status_text: No new data. SWIFT's legacy network remains dominant at 11,500 banks. Hormuz disruption may accelerate corridor diversification but SWIFT benefits from incumbent position during crises.

competing_infrastructure.name: Visa B2B Connect
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Visa expanding stablecoin-linked cards from 18 to 100+ countries with Stripe partnership. This is direct competition for cross-border payment rails that Ripple targets. Visa's distribution advantage is formidable.

competing_infrastructure.name: JPMorgan Kinexys
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Dimon's 'stablecoins as banks' comment signals JPMorgan positioning to capture regulated stablecoin market. Kinexys has built-in institutional client base. No new deployment data.

competing_infrastructure.name: BIS Project Nexus
competing_infrastructure.status_text: No new developments detected. BIS mandate for sovereign settlement control remains the structural threat to private bridge currencies. Geopolitical fragmentation could accelerate BIS regional alternatives.

competing_infrastructure.name: Ethereum Institutional
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Ondo tokenized securities on Binance ADGM represent Ethereum-ecosystem institutional RWA adoption. DTCC positioning as digital asset foundation could favor Ethereum's established institutional tooling over XRPL. Adjacent but real competitive pressure on RWA thesis.

bear_case.odl_stagnation: ODL volume data remains completely unavailable — the kill switch cannot be evaluated. Ripple's $100B+ cumulative figure is lifetime and unverifiable against annualized ODL-specific metrics. This opacity is itself a bear signal.
bear_case.token_velocity_concern: No token velocity data available. XRP 24h volume at $3.28B against $83.1B market cap suggests ~4% daily turnover — not unusual for a large-cap crypto but provides no insight into institutional hold patterns vs. speculative cycling.

macro_headwinds.name: Global Recession Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Hormuz closure + US-Spain trade cutoff + Section 122 tariffs = active trade destruction. Oil supply shock feeds directly into recession risk. Extreme fear at 14 reflects market pricing of this scenario.

macro_headwinds.name: Crypto Winter Signals
macro_headwinds.status_text: XRP -3% today, broad crypto -3.3%, F&G at 14. Not yet a crypto winter but the geopolitical overlay creating sustained risk-off could trigger one if Hormuz crisis extends beyond weeks.

macro_headwinds.name: Regulatory Reversal Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: CFTC perpetual futures approval and continued ETF operation suggest regulatory direction remains favorable. Dimon's bank-regulation-for-stablecoins comment bears watching but is not reversal.

macro_headwinds.name: Rate Hike Extension
macro_headwinds.status_text: US 10Y at 3.97% (stale data from Feb 27). Oil supply shock could reignite inflation expectations, complicating Fed's rate path. If Brent sustains above $90, rate cut timeline extends significantly.

bear_case.bear_narrative: CRITICAL compound stress declared per framework rules — Hormuz disruption event with cascading second-order effects (Iraq Rumaila shutdown, US-Spain trade cutoff) — though Brent at $82.88 challenges the severity of the disruption. Call is INCREASE_MONITORING, not REDUCE_EXPOSURE, because the thesis paradox is fully engaged: the geopolitical fragmentation threatening XRP's price is simultaneously the strongest structural argument for neutral settlement infrastructure. The dominant risk is not the crisis — it's the blindness. Three of five kill switches are unmeasurable, Ripple's ODL-to-Payments rebrand strategically retired the thesis's primary volume metric, and we cannot verify whether extreme fear is producing ETF outflows because AUM data is null. The thesis is not falsified but it is operating at dangerously reduced visibility. Operator action window is days, not weeks.
